title: Hugging Face Model Card Template Data
description: JSON Schema for the template variables used by huggingface_hub/templates/modelcard_template.md.

model_id.description: Display identifier or name of the model.

model_summary.description: Short summary shown near the top of the model card.

developers.description: Person, team, or organization that developed the model.

funded_by.description: Optional funding source for the model.

shared_by.description: Optional person, team, or organization that shared the model.

language.description: Language or languages relevant to the model.

license.description: License identifier or license text reference.

base_model.description: Optional source model used for fine-tuning or adaptation.

paper.description: Optional paper URL or citation reference.

direct_use.description: Primary intended uses of the model.

downstream_use.description: Optional downstream or derivative use cases.

out_of_scope_use.description: Uses that should be considered out of scope.

bias_recommendations.description: Recommendations for safe or appropriate use.

get_started_code.description: Code snippet or instructions for getting started with the model.

speeds_sizes_times.description: Optional training or inference speeds, model sizes, or timing information.

testing_data.description: Description of testing or evaluation data.

testing_metrics.description: Metrics used to evaluate the model.

model_examination.description: Optional analysis or examination of model behavior.

hardware_type.description: Hardware used for training or relevant environmental impact estimates.

model_card_authors.description: Optional author or authors of the model card.

model_card_contact.description: Contact information for the model card.

examples.model_summary: A concise summary of the model.
examples.model_description: A longer description covering what the model does and why it exists.
examples.direct_use: Use for classifying customer support tickets.
examples.out_of_scope_use: Do not use for medical diagnosis.
examples.training_data: Trained on a curated set of public support conversations.
examples.testing_data: Held-out support conversations from the same domain.
examples.results: Macro F1 reached 0.91 on the held-out set.
